name: Juan Rodriguez, kamalaharris.org
subject: Can you take a quick minute to complete this survey for Kamala?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
Hey, Juan here, Kamala’s campaign manager. We’re working on putting together our campaign plan for the rest of the spring, and I want to make sure your voice is at the table when we do. **[Can you take a quick minute to complete our survey so I can get a sense of your top priorities right now and what you’re looking to hear from candidates during the first debate in June?](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190503-may-survey?refcode=ema190505-c&source=ema190505-c&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1&akid=4652%2E5755789%2EDb2wTo)** | | [ Take the survey ](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190503-may-survey?refcode=ema190505-c&source=ema190505-c&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=2&akid=4652%2E5755789%2EDb2wTo)
---
I’m sure you saw Kamala grill Attorney General Barr in the Senate this week. That’s the kind of integrity -- and fearlessness -- we’re missing from our elected leaders right now.
Not surprisingly, Trump has already gone on the attack. It’s clear he’s afraid of having to debate Kamala in 2020 and facing questions like the ones Barr did this week.
**Kamala’s back on the trail a lot this month, and I have to say, the energy is just incredible. Folks are fired up to hear from Kamala and learn more about her vision for our country.**
About how we can give teachers the raise they deserve, pass a tax cut for the middle class and take real action to keep our communities safe from guns.
We’re off to a great start, but we have to do everything we can to build up our organization in the next few months. We have to convince voters in communities across the country that Kamala is the candidate to take on Trump and deliver for the people.
**[If you have ideas on things we should focus on or just want to share your story with Kamala, please complete our quick survey. I want to hear what you have to say.](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190503-may-survey?refcode=ema190505-c&source=ema190505-c&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=3&akid=4652%2E5755789%2EDb2wTo)**
| [ Take the survey ](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190503-may-survey?refcode=ema190505-c&source=ema190505-c&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=4&akid=4652%2E5755789%2EDb2wTo)
---
Thanks so much,
— Juan
Juan Rodriguez
Campaign Manager
Kamala 2020

PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: I just need your signature
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
Can I count on you to add your name before midnight and show your support for my campaign? No one thought we could make the cut, but we just showed the world what a real grassroots movement looks like | | |
---
| |
---
| | **BREAKING: Juli an Castro just made history and secured his spot in the Presidential Debates. **_[**Sign on to show your support for this historic campaign for President >>**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsH/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")_ | Hours ago, I became one of the few candidates to officially secure my spot in the Presidential debates.
No one thought we could get here, but you helped me prove them all wrong.
_[**Will you add your name to show your support for our historic movement? >>**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsE/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")_
---

I was never supposed to be a frontrunner in this presidential race.

I was never supposed to make the cut for the debate stage.

People who grew up where I did -- who were raised by single moms like I was -- who come from a family of immigrants like I do -- we were told to set our sights lower.

But you just helped me make history and prove them all wrong.

Because I just became one of the handful of candidates to reach the 65,000-donor requirement to officially secure my spot in the Presidential Debates.

[_**I'm asking you to add your name to show your support and help me prove that even an underdog like me can become President. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

Serving in President Obama's cabinet was one of the greatest honors of my life.

We fought for justice. We worked to improve lives. We strived to make this a country where people could live with dignity.

But watching Trump rip our progress to shreds has been the most infuriating spectacle I've ever witnessed.

I can't wait to get onto that debate stage and defend President Obama and the progress we made -- and it's all because of you.

So I'm humbly asking: [_**Can I count on you to add your name before midnight and show your support for my campaign? No one thought we could make the cut, but we just showed the world what a real grassroots movement looks like. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsC/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

When the establishment said I was a longshot...

When the press didn't give me a fair shot...

When the far-right said no one like me could ever be President...

You believed in me, and you had my back. You have no idea how much that means.

Thank you for believing that an underdog like me could get to the Democratic debate stage and share my vision with America.

[_**Thank you for giving me the opportunity to tell my story and earn your trust. And if you're still reading this, I hope I 've also earned your signature. So I'm humbly asking one last time: **_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsD/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")[_**Will you sign your name before midnight? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd73/3eb3e2fc/4e4bc86d/7449a3dc/2383004301/VEsA/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190505_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

Thank you,

Julian

name: Dan Baer
subject: Notes from the trail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
I'm not asking for money or for you to sign a petition. Just an update from the trail. Let me know what you think --------------
|
---
[ ](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82695/232dfd1/91354b7/62a15a62/3596037828/VEsH/ "https://www.danforcolorado.com")

| | _**This email is a longer one -- but many of you have asked for updates on how the campaign is going, and I wanted to share a bit about what I 've been up to. I'm not asking for money or for you to sign a petition or anything like that. Just an update from the trail. I hope you'll give it a read and let me know what you think.**_ I've spent the last week traveling the state of Colorado. I knew from day one I wanted this campaign to be different. Instead of starting with speeches, I wanted to start with questions. And I want to hear from everyone in every corner of this state. So after I announced my candidacy, I immediately hit the road. I've been doing what I'm calling a "Colorado at Work" tour -- in each place I go I am trying to spend quality time with people in their workplace, learning about their job, working alongside them when I can, and listening. Work is, for most of us, a central part of our lives -- it's how we make ends meet (or try to); it's something we juggle alongside family, school, community commitments. It's how we contribute and -- if we are lucky -- it's more than a job, it's a source of purpose or fulfillment, of dignity. That's why I decided to focus my first visits in communities around the state on working people, on understanding the hopes and struggles of Coloradans at work. **First, I went to eastern Colorado. ** Now, I realize that's not where Democrats usually start their campaigns in Colorado. But, especially in these first few months, I want to be more focused on listening than talking -- and I want to listen to everyone across the state. I don't expect to agree with everything I hear, but I expect to learn how to better represent all of Colorado. Eastern Colorado is historically more conservative than the Front Range / Denver metro area. But I'm not running to represent the city of Denver. I'm running to represent Colorado, and I want to hear from these communities at the outset. I want to listen to their stories and learn about the challenges and opportunities facing people from all across our great state. Our first stop was in Yuma, and I took the opportunity to drop in on my friend Don Brown. I had grown fond of Don's reports in Governor Hickenlooper's cabinet meetings when he served as Commissioner of Agriculture and I was leading the Department of Higher Education. What I liked about Don was that he wasn't just an advocate for the issues facing Colorado's agricultural communities. He was and is a part of an agricultural community -- his family has been farming in Yuma county for over 100 years. Now Don's a Republican, and I am a Democrat -- so this wasn't a political meeting. But it was a chance for me to ask a lot of questions, and for him to share how economic and regulatory changes have affected farmers like him, and what he sees as the key challenges in the years ahead. We went out to check on Don's son as he was planting corn. I rode along in the tractor for a while, learning about the ways in which planting has become a precision operation. Don shared a favorite quote with me: "There's no bigger expert than someone who hasn't done it for a living." I don't pretend to be an expert -- but I certainly appreciated the opportunity to listen and learn. **Not long after, we hopped in the pickup and headed down to Pueblo, where my great-grandfather was born.** A student I met at CSU-Pueblo told me that the job of a politician is "to make people's lives better." If you want to do that, the first step is to actually learn about their lives. In Pueblo, I had the privilege of meeting with Katrina, a CSU-Pueblo student who wants to work with troubled youth when she graduates. But to do so, she has to take out tens of thousands of dollars in loans -- which leaves her worried about whether she'll be able to afford to do the service work she's training for and wants to do. "I'll probably owe money until the day I die," she said. "And if I don't find a job, I'll still have these loans." I got to spend a little time at one of Katrina's jobs. She's working full time and going to school full time. She's been a delivery driver for Little Caesar's and she's working at Chili's in the kitchen. But on campus she works at the CSU-Pueblo writing center, helping fellow students structure papers and improve their writing. Together with the other staff, we talked about how they juggle their studies and work. We talked about how rewarding they find it to know they are helping other students -- many of whom are ESL students -- learn the communication skills they will need to get and keep good jobs. (And helping them get through school and get the degree they came for!) I also met with Kyle, a Licensed Practical Nurse. I got a haircut from and chatted with Harvest at a local barber shop / hair salon. And I finished off the day with a HUGE meal of stuffed sopapillas at Estela's Mill Stop Cafe -- a local staple run by Mark and his three brothers. Mark talked to me about how health care costs have skyrocketed and how he wants to keep prices low, but rising costs make that a challenge. People in Pueblo, whether they were born here, or they moved here: They love this community. Not because it's perfect -- they know there are social and economic challenges --- but because it is a community with history and a soul. That came through in almost every conversation. For the sake of email sanity, I'll stop there -- but we'll have more stories from the trail in the coming weeks as we document our travels across Colorado to Alamosa, Durango, Grand Junction, Fort Collins, and beyond. If you're not already doing so, I'd love it if you would follow along on [Facebook](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82695/232dfd1/91354b7/62a15a63/3596037828/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/"), [Instagram](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82695/232dfd1/91354b7/62a15a60/3596037828/VEsF/ "https://www.instagram.com/danforcolorado/"), or [Twitter](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82695/232dfd1/91354b7/62a15a61/3596037828/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/danbbaer"). I know this race won't be easy. I know I have a lot more of Colorado to see, and many more challenges ahead that I can't even imagine yet. But I also know that I have people like you behind me -- and that's the greatest honor of my life. Thank you for reading and for your support, Dan | | [**DONATE**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82695/232dfd1/91354b7/62a15a66/3596037828/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190505_em_fr_b1_baer_all&recur_weekly=on&recurring=on&amount=25")

name: Faiz Shakir
subject: [Enclosed] Bernie Sanders for President Strategy Memo
body:
Re: 2020 Strategy Memo
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw)

To: Bernie Sanders supporters
From: Faiz Shakir, Campaign Manager
Re: 2020 Strategy Memo
Action: Contribute $3 to help us execute our plan [**(LINK TO CONTRIBUTE)** ](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11478?refcode=em190505-1-v1-subs&t=2&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw)
* * *
**The State of our Campaign**
The question on voters’ minds this primary election is not only which campaign gives us the best chance to defeat Donald Trump, but who is the candidate people can actually trust to fight to transform the political and economic life in this country?
On those questions, the answer is pretty clear:
Bernie Sanders and our campaign are our country’s best chance at both. And the good news is we are off to the kind of start that will get the job done.
**A Grassroots Campaign**
What we have learned over the past few years is that no one candidate can take on Donald Trump, the Republican Party and the billionaire class of this country alone.
If we are going to win this election and transform our country, it will require the largest grassroots movement this country has ever seen.
And in that respect, no one comes close to what we are building so far:
**April 27, 2019**
A little less than two weeks ago, our supporters led almost 5,000 organizing kick-off meetings attended by more than 60,000 people. It was a series of events that far surpassed anything any campaign has done, or is even capable of at this point. And it should serve as another data point that ours is by far the strongest grassroots campaign in this race.
At each one of these kick-off meetings, people heard from me, Bernie, Senator Nina Turner, and then discussed our strategy to organize and win this election.
Everyone was given marching orders on the best way to identify new potential supporters. And since that day, we have identified almost 50,000 new people who we’ll work to register to vote and then turn out to the polls.
And we’re just getting started.
**Unrivaled Small-Dollar Fundraising**
Campaigns cost an obscene amount of money. By some estimates, a winning campaign will probably have to raise $1 billion to defeat Donald Trump. It’s grotesque, for sure. Not exactly an indication of a healthy democratic process.
But the truth is, there is a limit to the number of max-out checks available to candidates. And raising the kind of money it will take to win requires a large, grassroots fundraising operation.
There is just no other way.
The fact that we have FAR more donors and donations than any other candidate in this race shows that we are the campaign in the best position to pull it off. That’s because Bernie is the kind of candidate who inspires participation.
And why? Because you can trust him to fight.
**People Trust Bernie Sanders to Fight**
The reason we are doing well with the grassroots in this campaign is because Bernie is telling the truth about the reality of life in America today, and because people trust him to fight for the bold structural changes we need.
If we are going to defeat Trump, it is only going to happen with a candidate who has a record of consistency on the issues important to the working people of this country.
Voters can see right through the candidates who say one thing to them but are clearly running to serve the needs of the ruling class in this country.
With Bernie, you **_never**_ have to worry if that is the case.
**Polling**
The polling bears this out as well. In survey after survey, including our own, one of the reasons people gravitate most to Bernie is that they see him as someone they trust to fight for the needs of the working people in America.
And in contrast to Trump, that is especially true in three of the most important states we have to win in order to capture the White House.
Check out these recent polling numbers:
**Michigan**
Sanders 52% to Trump 41%
**Wisconsin**
Sanders 52% to Trump 42%
**Pennsylvania**
Sanders 51% to Trump 43%
Win these three states and we probably win the White House. And for all of the reasons mentioned above, Bernie gives us the best chance to get it done.
**Our work in the states**
Of course, we have to win the primary before we can defeat Donald Trump, so I wanted to give you an update on the team we’re building in the states to get it done.
Right now, we are building our teams in what is generally referred to as “the First Five” states: Iowa, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Nevada, and California — places people vote and caucus before anywhere else.
And in each of the states, direct voter contact is already underway. We are talking to voters and leaders in each state, mobilizing volunteers, and building the kind of campaigns that will help us win.
And it’s working. Just a few weeks ago in South Carolina — a state we are going to do very well in this time — we received a slate of endorsements from eight elected officials, faith and labor leaders from across the state.
I am also excited to share with you that we are starting to build out our team in Super Tuesday states. Our ability to do that before other candidates is a direct result of your grassroots fundraising support.
It gives us an enormous advantage, and positions us well to win.
**Conclusion**
Like we said from the start, this is a campaign that is built to win.
We have the best candidate in the race — one who leads on the issues the American people care about, and who they can trust to fight for the promises he makes on the campaign trail.
We also have the strongest grassroots organization of any candidate in the Democratic primary. That’s what we’re going to need if we’re going to defeat Donald Trump and fight to transform this country after the ballots are counted.
And the truth is, we are in this strong position for one reason:
You.
Bernie said it best on launch day: no one candidate can take on Donald Trump and the billionaire class of this country alone. There is only one way — and that is together.
Not me. Us.
Anything we’ve accomplished so far and anything we accomplish going forward is because we are bringing people together — people from all backgrounds and in all communities — to fight for the change we so desperately need.
Thank you for being a critical part of that effort. And thank you for reading.
In solidarity,
Faiz Shakir
Campaign Manager
* * *
**If you can make a $3 contribution today, we’ll put it right to work executing our plan to win this primary and defeat Donald Trump. But we can’t do that without you. So please, this is important.**
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11478?refcode=em190505-1-v1-subs&t=3&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=5&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=6&refcode2=1261_150750_QMfscw&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=7&akid=1261%2E150750%2EQMfscw).

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name to support my new plan to improve military housing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Let's fight to make sure our service members can count on decent housing. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f894/45c30650/80ab3a47/669635d8/776285451/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
All three of my big brothers served in the military.

I know the responsibilities we owe to our service members, veterans, and their families.

So I've got a new plan to improve military housing, and I'd like to take a minute to tell you about it.

The way it works right now, a handful of private developers have taken over 99% of domestic military family housing.

Driven by short-term payoffs and with little meaningful oversight, these contractors have failed to maintain the homes they were entrusted with.

Military families have been living with lead paint, faulty wiring, collapsing ceilings, and mice infestations. Mold has taken over entire rooms. Kids are getting sick from living in these homes.

That's not how we should treat our service members -- and it needs to stop now.

**[My plan would improve our military housing, protect families from abuse, and hold private developers accountable. Add your name if you're ready to be a part of this fight.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f894/45c30650/80ab3a47/669635db/776285451/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwNWVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-militaryhousing?source=20190505em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Here's how it'll work.

Let's start with something simple: If a developer doesn't live up to the terms of its agreement to maintain habitable properties for our military families, we shouldn't reward them with bonuses or other incentive payments.

In fact, I'll require the Secretary of Defense to standardize leases across the military services and review all existing housing contracts for violations before they can be renewed.

Under my proposal, every base will have a housing office staffed with advocates for service members -- not beholden to private contractors. That office will have independent authority to inspect housing on base to ensure that it's safe, clean, and meets all state and local requirements.

It'll be illegal for any senior defense official -- or any member of Congress who oversees them -- to benefit from investing in a military housing company with business before the Pentagon.

Military tenants will get a "bill of rights," in writing, when they move in to protect them from abuse.

First on the list: Families can withhold payment when landlords don't play by the rules.

Second: If a service member's home needs repairs or remediation, the work order can't be closed until the service member approves. And if the family has to stay somewhere else during repairs, the landlord pays for that, too -- and in a worst case scenario, a service member should be able to move off base without penalty.

There'll be one standardized resident satisfaction survey across all the services -- conducted independently from the housing provider -- and one consolidated database of resident complaints.

We'll make all those results public to make sure every military family can make an informed choice about where to live when they move.

We also need to take care of families who've been harmed.

The impacts of substandard housing can linger for a lifetime. My plan requires the Pentagon to establish a health registry for service members and their families, and to screen and track for medical conditions they got as a result of unsafe housing. And my plan would provide lifetime care for any dependent child who was harmed by substandard housing.

The sacrifices our service members, veterans, and their families make -- constant moves, repeated deployments -- are enormous.

The way I see it, this isn't complicated -- it's not even a close call: **No matter where they are stationed, the very least we owe our military personnel is a safe, affordable place to live.**

If we can't provide that, it's bad for morale, it's bad for retention, it's bad for readiness -- but most importantly, it's a breach of trust owed to those who volunteer to defend our country.

**[Add your name if you're with me, and let's fight to make sure our service members can count on decent housing.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f894/45c30650/80ab3a47/669635db/776285451/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwNWVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-militaryhousing?source=20190505em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

Peter, I joined Joe Biden's campaign because we're at a pivotal moment in our country's history and I couldn't sit on the sidelines. Everything we do right now is absolutely critical.

Over two years with Donald Trump in the White House has moved this country backwards and left painful scars on our nation's soul. Families have been separated, with children lost in the system. Hate became a new standard as neo-Nazis and Klansmen were given a platform by this President.

So to give him four more years? No. That will fundamentally change the entire fabric of our nation. I don't accept that, Peter. Neither should you.

I'm on Team Joe because I trust him to get this right. I trust him to run a campaign that has standards and values and a commitment to win the White House.
